title: syzygium glanduligerum
intro: Syzygium glanduligerum, also known as the stinkwood, is a tropical tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive and pungent odor. The tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Syzygium glanduligerum include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its bark and leaves can be used to make herbal teas, tinctures, or capsules for various health benefits.
